info.name: Acme Cart API
info.description:
Multi-tenant cart system with two parallel API surfaces:

**Storefront** (customer-facing). Every cart endpoint operates on the authenticated customer's single active cart -- there is no cart_id in the URL because customer_id (from X-Customer-Id) determines the cart uniquely. Auth is the `X-Customer-Id` header set by the upstream identity proxy.

**Admin** (operator-facing). Lives under `/api/v1/admin/`. Uses DRF token auth, not X-Customer-Id, so the two rails never share credentials. Tenant-admin endpoints run under the tenant's subdomain (RLS still applies); platform-admin endpoints run under `admin.acme.test` (BYPASSRLS via the `admin` DB alias).

All requests inherit headers from collection variables (`customerId`, `tenantHost`, `adminToken`, `adminHost`). Resource IDs are captured automatically by response-test scripts.

**Recommended order (storefront):** Health → List Products → Add Address → Add Payment Method → Add Item → Set Shipping Address → Set Billing Address → Set Payment Method → Checkout → Get Order → Get Invoice.

**Recommended order (admin):** Admin - Auth → Login (Tenant) → any Admin - Tenant call. Or Admin - Auth → Login (Platform) → any Admin - Platform call.

request.description: List active products for the current tenant. Captures the first product's id into the `productId` collection variable for downstream requests.

query.description: Case-insensitive substring across sku, name, description.
Exact SKU match (still tenant-scoped). Disabled by default.
When true, only products with availableQuantity > 0.

request.description: Same endpoint as List Products with all available filters wired up. Disable any param via the query-row checkbox in Postman to exclude it from the request. All filters are AND-combined.
Retrieve a single product by id. Returns 404 if inactive or cross-tenant. Run **List Products** first to populate the `productId` collection variable.
List the authenticated customer's saved addresses. Bare list (no envelope). Default-first ordering.

exec: if (id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('addressId', id); console.log('Captured addressId =', id); }

request.description: Retrieve a single saved address. 404 if not owned by the customer.
Partial update. Use PUT for a full replace.
Hard delete. Fails if still referenced by an active cart slot (clear the slot first). Orders snapshot their addresses so historical orders are unaffected.

Discovery: which gateways does this tenant's storefront accept? Returns only active configs; the `mock` gateway is hidden when `PAYMENTS_ALLOW_MOCK_GATEWAY=False` (default in prod). Call this before Add Payment Method to know which `gatewayName` values are valid — the response captures the default into the `gatewayName` collection variable.

Detail / capabilities for one gateway. Same `name` / `displayName` / `isDefault` as the list, plus `supportedCurrencies` (empty = accept all), `tokenization` (`client` for Stripe.js-style, `server` for the mock), `supports3ds`, and `publicCredentials` (e.g. Stripe's `publishableKey`). Secrets like `secretKey` / `webhookSecret` are intentionally never in the response.

Returns 404 if the gateway isn't configured for this tenant or if the `mock` gate is off — same behaviour as the list endpoint, so iterating the list is safe.
List saved payment methods. Tokens are redacted to brand + lastFour; the raw gateway token is never returned.

`gatewayName` must be one of the enum values (mock / stripe / hyperpay / tap) — anything else returns 400 with a field-level validation error. The gateway must also be configured and active for this tenant — otherwise 409 `gateway_not_configured`. `mock` is rejected when `PAYMENTS_ALLOW_MOCK_GATEWAY=False`. Run `List Payment Gateways` first to populate `{{gatewayName}}` from the tenant's actual config.

B2B purchase-order payment method. Skips the gateway tokenize step — no `gatewayName` / `token` / `brand`. `paymentTerms` is required and snapshotted onto the order at checkout (one of `net_15` / `net_30` / `net_60` / `net_90`); `payment_due_date` is computed from it.

Restricted to B2B customers: a B2C `customerId` returns `403 forbidden`. The seeded B2B customer is Diana (`{{b2bCustomerId}}`); to exercise this path swap `customerId → b2bCustomerId` for the rest of the storefront flow (addresses, cart items, Set Payment Method, Checkout). The checkout response will return `paymentStatus: "invoice_pending"` and `status: 202`, and the order stays in `pending` until `Mark Order Paid (admin)`.
Atomically clear any existing default and set this one. Idempotent.
Hard delete. Fails if still referenced by an active cart slot.
Empty the active cart and mark it abandoned. Idempotent — returns 204 even if there was no active cart. The next Add Item lazy-creates a fresh cart.
Replace the line's quantity (does not increment — for that use POST /cart/items). Quantity 0 is rejected; use DELETE to remove. 409 insufficient_stock if quantity > availableQuantity.
Dry-run validation of a coupon code. Returns { valid, discount, projectedGrandTotal, ... } without persisting an AppliedCoupon or bumping cart version. Same failure codes as Apply Coupon.

exec: if (orderId) { pm.collectionVariables.set('orderId', orderId); console.log('Captured orderId =', orderId); }

query.description: Filter by status: pending | paid | fulfilled | cancelled | refunded. Omit for all.

request.description: Paginated order history for the authenticated customer, newest first.

Returns the invoice envelope: invoiceNumber (per-tenant sequence), pdfUrl (real PDF in MinIO/S3 — populated by the same Celery task that creates the row, may be `""` for a few hundred ms while the upload finishes), and issuedAt. Rendering is async, so this can 404 for ~1s after checkout — poll until it returns 200 with a non-empty pdfUrl, then run 'Download Invoice PDF' below to fetch the file itself.

Fetches the rendered invoice PDF directly from object storage. In dev that's MinIO at http://localhost:9000/invoices/...; in prod it's whatever AWS_S3_ENDPOINT_URL points at. The bucket is public-read in dev (anonymous download policy), so no signing is needed and Postman shows the PDF inline in the response preview. Run 'Get Invoice' first to populate the invoicePdfUrl variable.

Cancel a PENDING order: releases inventory reservations and marks any authorized payment as cancelled. Idempotent. 409 order_not_cancellable if the order is past the cancellable window (already paid or fulfilled — use refund instead).
Webhooks are exempt from auth and tenant resolution; the tenant is derived from the Payment row matched by gateway_transaction_id. This request mimics a Stripe-shaped event payload.

item.name: Admin - Auth
item.description: Shared auth surface for both tenant-admin and platform-admin. Issues / revokes DRF tokens. Login is host-independent (in GLOBAL_EXEMPT_PATHS) -- you can run it against tenantHost or adminHost; only the subsequent calls care.

request.description: Exchange tenant-admin email + password for a DRF token. The token is captured into `adminToken` and reused by every Admin - Tenant request. Wrong credentials and unknown emails both return `401 invalid_credentials` (no user enumeration).

Exchange platform-admin email + password for a DRF token. The token is captured into `adminToken` and reused by every Admin - Platform request. Superusers are also accepted by `IsTenantAdmin`, so you can use this token for Admin - Tenant calls too (useful for support escalations).
Returns the current admin's id, email, isSuperuser flag, and tenant memberships. Superusers see `memberships: []`; tenant admins see one entry per (tenant, role) row.
Deletes the token row. Subsequent calls with this token return 401. Re-login via the Login requests to refresh.

item.name: Admin - Tenant
item.description:
Per-tenant admin surface. Runs under the tenant's subdomain (`tenantHost`), so RLS still scopes queries to the resolved tenant. A tenant-admin token issued for store-a returns 403 when used against store-b.

Log in via 'Admin - Auth → Login (Tenant Admin)' first to populate `adminToken`.

request.description: List products for the resolved tenant. Unlike the storefront list, includes inactive products too so admins can re-activate them.

Create a product on the resolved tenant. `tenant_id` is auto-populated from the request's tenant context (no need to pass it). The captured `adminProductId` is reused by the detail / update / delete requests below.
Soft delete: flips `isActive=false`. Hard delete is refused because Product is referenced by historical CartItem and OrderItem rows.

formdata.description: PNG / JPEG / WebP, max 5 MiB. Click 'Select Files' in Postman.

request.description: Multipart POST setting the product's primary image. Returns the full admin product including `imageKey` (stable storage key) and `imageUrl` (public URL via the `media` bucket on MinIO/S3). Re-uploading overwrites the existing object in place; changing format (png → webp) writes the new key and drops the old object automatically. Validation: png/jpeg/webp content-type, ≤ 5 MiB.

Fetches the rendered product image directly from object storage. The bucket is public-read in dev (anonymous download policy), so no auth or signing is needed. Run 'Upload Product Image' first to populate productImageUrl.
Clears the product's image: deletes the object from the media bucket and resets `image_key` to empty. Returns 204. The product row itself is untouched.
Create a coupon. Code must be unique per tenant. `discountType` is `percentage` or `fixed`; fixed-discount coupons must specify `currency` matching the cart currency.
Code is immutable (existing carts and orders reference it). Everything else is mutable.
Soft delete (`isActive=false`). Preserves order history.
Setting `isDefault: true` clears the existing default for this tenant in the same transaction (only one default gateway per tenant is allowed).
Hard delete. `PaymentMethod.gateway_config` is `on_delete=PROTECT`, so configs that still have saved methods will 500 — clean those up first.

Lists every order in the resolved tenant (storefront `/orders` is filtered to the calling customer). Optional `?status=` and `?customer_id=` filters — enable in the query tab.

Each row uses `AdminOrderSerializer`, so it carries `customerId`, the full `payments` array (newest first, with `gatewayName` + `gatewayTransactionId` + `gatewayResponse` for reconciliation), and the linked `invoice` block. Scan for `status: "paid"` rows with an empty `invoice.pdfUrl` — those are the orders where the post-payment Celery task hasn't completed.

Tenant admins can fetch any order in their tenant for support; the storefront `/orders/{id}` is restricted to the calling customer.

Uses `AdminOrderSerializer`: response includes `customerId`, nested `payments[]` (every Payment attempt with gateway txn ids + raw response), and `invoice` (`pdfUrl` non-empty = the post-payment chain succeeded end-to-end).

Dedicated payment-attempt history for one order, newest first. Same data as the nested `payments[]` on Get Order, but useful when the order page is heavy and you just need to confirm capture happened.

Response fields per row: `status`, `amount` + `currency`, `gatewayName`, `gatewayTransactionId` (the id to paste into Stripe/HyperPay dashboards), `idempotencyKey`, `gatewayResponse` (raw gateway JSON — admin-only, contains failure codes / last4 / sometimes IPs), `createdAt`, `updatedAt`.

Tenant-admin flips a purchase-order (PO) order from `PENDING` to `PAID` once the invoice clears. Only valid for orders whose payment is in `INVOICE_PENDING` — the PO path through checkout. Card orders already get to `PAID` via the gateway capture flow; calling this on a card order is a no-op (returns the order unchanged) rather than an error.

Idempotent — a second call on an already-PAID order returns the order as-is. The optional `reference` (wire id, cheque number, anything) is stored on `Payment.gateway_response.marked_paid_reference` for the audit trail.
List Customer rows for the tenant. Includes soft-deleted (isActive=false) rows too. Optional filters in the query tab — enable to narrow.

Creates the application record only — the upstream identity proxy must also know about the email before the customer can log in. Useful for B2B pre-provisioning. B2B customers must include taxId + companyName; B2C may omit them. Duplicate emails on the same tenant return 422.
Email is immutable. PATCH isActive=false to block the customer, isActive=true to unblock.
Same effect as the DELETE request below — CustomerAuthMiddleware refuses X-Customer-Id lookups for inactive customers (401 customer_not_found, no enumeration).
Soft delete: flips isActive=false. Hard delete is impossible because Order references Customer with on_delete=PROTECT.

item.name: Admin - Platform
item.description:
Cross-tenant ops. Runs under `admin.acme.test`, with tenant resolution skipped (GLOBAL_EXEMPT_PATHS) and ORM routed through the `app_admin` DB alias (BYPASSRLS). Requires `User.is_superuser=True`.

Log in via 'Admin - Auth → Login (Platform Admin)' first to populate `adminToken`.

request.description: Creates the tenant row, then provisions per-tenant Postgres sequences (`order_number_seq_<uuid>`, `invoice_number_seq_<uuid>`) on the admin DB alias. Reserved subdomains (www, api, admin, static, media) are rejected with 422.
Subdomain is immutable: it's the routing identity. Changing it would break in-flight URLs, customer bookmarks, and any gateway webhook callbacks registered against the old hostname.

Bind a Django User to the new tenant as a tenant-admin. `createUserIfMissing: true` provisions the User inline using `initialPassword` (which the new admin should rotate) and requires `firstName` + `lastName`. Idempotent: assigning the same (user, tenant) twice updates the role rather than 409-ing.
Hard-delete the membership row. The user's existing token (if any) survives until their next admin request, which then 401s because `IsTenantAdmin` re-checks membership on every call.

Cross-tenant customer search for Zid support. Mandatory ?email= (substring match) so the endpoint can't accidentally dump the whole customer table; missing email returns 400 email_query_required. Each row in the response includes tenantSubdomain so support can hop into the right store. Tenant-admin tokens get 403 here (IsPlatformAdmin requires superuser).
